$comment: Reference case for dof_coc (CoC = abs(aperture*focalLength*(focusDistance-depth) / (depth*(focusDistance-focalLength)))). Three coefficients, but as a function of `depth` alone they collapse to two effective parameters (CoC = A/depth - B), so aperture/focalLength/focusDistance are individually non-identifiable. This case asserts fit quality only. The depth sweep stays on [1,4] m — one side of the abs() kink at depth=focusDistance=5 — so the fitted function is smooth.
notes: 25-point depth sweep ∈ [1,4] m, kept below focusDistance=5 so the abs() never folds (the argument stays positive) and the curve is the smooth 1/depth branch. CoC runs ≈0.0071–0.113 (×1e-3 m disk). No coefficient bounds: the thin-lens coefficients alias to two DOF parameters when only depth varies — a real regression surfaces as an R²/RMSE breach.
